summary.intent: Fix two bugs in the browser session viewer: sessions dying from connection errors were never appearing in session history, and rapid session switching was causing WebSocket relay accumulation that froze the UI.
summary.outcome: Both bugs were fixed and pushed: the history UI now shows all past sessions (not just those with closed_at set), and cleanup_stale_session was updated to mark sessions closed in the database when they die from connection errors.

repo: Sessions that die from connection errors go through cleanup_stale_session in crates/browser/src/manager.rs, which removed them from the pool but did not update the database closed_at field.
The history UI in page-browser.js filtered sessions by presence of closed_at, which excluded sessions that died abnormally.
start_screencast spawns a new WebSocket relay each call; without a corresponding stop_screencast, relays accumulate and duplicate frames flood the WebSocket causing freezes.
The fix for screencast accumulation was to check session state for screencasting: true before spawning a new relay, attaching the frame listener to the existing one instead.

code.finding: History filter was checking for closed_at presence; changed to filter out currently active sessions instead, so abnormally-terminated sessions appear in history.
cleanup_stale_session did not invoke the action hook to set closed_at in the database; fix adds that call so connection-error deaths are persisted.

workflow: Compiled and linted before committing to catch issues introduced by the Rust manager change.
Two separate bugs were addressed in one session: a UI filtering bug (JS) and a database update omission (Rust), committed together.

friction: Session disappearance from history was a compound bug requiring fixes in two separate layers (JS UI filter logic and Rust backend persistence), making it non-obvious to diagnose.
open_items: Line numbers for the specific edits in page-browser.js and manager.rs were not captured, so exact change locations are unknown for future reference.
No explicit test coverage mentioned for the screencast relay deduplication or the closed_at persistence path — these could regress silently.
